name: PostHog API
what_it_does: Open-source product analytics platform with event tracking, feature flags, session recordings, and A/B testing, plus an official MCP server for agent-driven analytics querying.
use_cases: Querying analytics events and insights via MCP server from agents
Evaluating feature flags in backend services
Capturing custom events from agent workflows
Fetching user cohorts and properties for targeting
Running A/B test experiments and reading results via API
not_for: Teams needing on-premise hosting without engineering resources (self-hosted needs maintenance)
Teams needing pre-built marketing automation (use Segment + downstream tools)
best_when: You want open-source product analytics with MCP server support and the option to self-host for data control.
avoid_when: You need enterprise SLA guarantees without self-hosting expertise or a pure marketing automation platform.

auth.notes: Personal API key for all API access. Project API key for event ingestion (public). Simple and agent-friendly.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 1M events/month free, 15k session recordings, unlimited feature flag evaluations
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-as-you-go after free tier: ~$0.00045/event, session recordings from $0.005 each
pricing.notes: Very generous free tier. Self-hosted version is completely free. Cloud pricing is usage-based after free tier. No seat-based pricing.

performance.rate_limits: API: varies by endpoint; feature flag evaluation optimized for high throughput

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Good error messages with detail fields. Open-source codebase allows debugging error paths.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Events can use uuid field for deduplication. Feature flag evaluations are stateless and idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: MCP server is community-maintained and may not cover all API endpoints
Feature flag evaluation requires distinct_id which must match user identity system
Self-hosted instances need manual version updates - can drift from cloud API behavior
HogQL (PostHog's SQL dialect) has limitations vs standard SQL for complex queries
Event ingestion is asynchronous - newly sent events not immediately queryable

security_score_components.security_notes: Personal API keys and project API keys. Project keys for event capture only. SOC2 Type II, GDPR. Self-hostable for full data control. Open-source core. EU Cloud option for data residency.
